# Nuon pkg generated code

We have a mono repo, and try to rely on code generation as much as possible. In the `ctl-api` we run `go run` and point
it at packages in here.

## Temporal Gen

Temporal-gen is a legacy system that uses annotations of temporal activities, to allow us to easily generate activity
and workflow wrappers. A good example is here:

For a workflow:

```go
// @temporal-gen workflow
// @execution-timeout 60m
// @task-timeout 30m
func (w *Workflows) ProcessJob(ctx workflow.Context, sreq signals.RequestSignal) error {
```

This generates:

```go
//  THIS FILE IS GENERATED. DO NOT EDIT. EDITING IS FUTILE.
//
//  Generated by:
//      mono/pkg/bins/temporal-gen
//  Using jennies:
//      WorkflowJenny
//
//  Run 'go generate' to regenerate this file.
//
//  slack @sdboyer about any problems in this file, we'll get em fixed in a jiffy

package worker

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/nuonco/mono/services/ctl-api/internal/app/runners/signals"
	"github.com/nuonco/mono/services/ctl-api/internal/pkg/eventloop"
	enumsv1 "go.temporal.io/api/enums/v1"
	"go.temporal.io/sdk/workflow"
)

// AwaitProcessJob executes [*Workflows.ProcessJob] as a Temporal workflow, waits for the future to complete, then returns the error, if any.
func AwaitProcessJob(ctx workflow.Context, req signals.RequestSignal) error {
	var _ = (&Workflows{}).ProcessJob
	// use this ^ for to go-to-definition jumping in your editor

	cwo := workflow.ChildWorkflowOptions{
		TaskQueue:                "api",
		WorkflowID:               eventloop.SignalHandlerWorkflowID(req.Signal, req.EventLoopRequest),
		WorkflowExecutionTimeout: 1 * time.Hour,
		WorkflowTaskTimeout:      30 * time.Minute,
		WorkflowIDReusePolicy:    enumsv1.WORKFLOW_ID_REUSE_POLICY_TERMINATE_IF_RUNNING,
		WaitForCancellation:      false,
	}
	ctx = workflow.WithChildOptions(ctx, cwo)

	// The func pointer being passed here is not used for execution. Temporal uses it as the
	// key for a name lookup against its activity registry, so that it knows what activity function
	// to actually call when the workflow is ready to be executed.
	return workflow.ExecuteChildWorkflow(ctx, (&Workflows{}).ProcessJob, req).Get(ctx, nil)
}
```

The idea is that with each method like this, you have a nice `Await` method you can then call natively, vs having to do
this.

We also support this for activities.

## Problem

The problem with `temporal-gen` is two fold: 1. it relies on a custom package from grafana we do not want to use
anymore. Secondly, we don't actually have an easy way to expand it. For instance, it currently does not work in an
individual repo, and it's pretty hard to expand.

## Solution

We want to build a new `temporal-gen` system that allows us to generate files. It should support all of the same
arguments as the current solution.

### steps

1. can you identify all the arguments and things we support today?
1. Can you help me identify the ways we can structure this code?
1. Let's break it down into steps.

### Additional Features

1. we should add the ability to send queries and updates via this system. I should be able to annotate an update handler
   and get a
1. we should be able to add customizations at _call_ time, so AwaitPRocessJob should take variadic arguments to
   customize things like the workflow-id, timeout and other things.
1. we should make sure that we can actually use it for crons and other things.

Also, I would like to make sure this is test driven. Can you build a good test suite that gives us a way to have sample
code that we validate or something like that?
